text: Rebuild idea-forge around a single thesis made structurally inescapable: the deliverable is a REINFORCEMENT LEDGER, and the tournament exists only to manufacture objections to feed it. Three concrete mechanism changes. (1) DUAL CARRY-FORWARD, JUDGE LAST. Kill the v1 order of "judge picks winner, then winner absorbs one objection." In every pair, BOTH variants first absorb the opponent's single strongest valid objection (mutual reinforcement); ONLY THEN does selection run, and it runs on the two already-reinforced texts. This makes the skill's name literally true — selection operates over reinforced ideas, not raw ones — and guarantees a reinforcement event on the winner's lineage every single round, eliminating the "winner advances unchanged / carry-forward was a no-op so the bracket was pointless" failure. (2) COLLAPSE THE JUDGE TO ONE AXIS THAT IS THE THESIS: "residual attack surface." After dual absorption the judge does not sum five criteria or quote top/bottom lines; it attempts to land ONE new fatal objection on each reinforced variant and advances the one with the smaller remaining attack surface, quoting the residual attack it could still land. The five v1 criteria are not scored — they become the objection TAXONOMY (an objection only counts as "fatal" if it threatens Impact, Feasibility, or Defensibility), so impact-killing attacks still win debates and the robustness-monoculture trap is closed without scoring ceremony. This also halves cost: per pair drops from 6 Opus calls (2 opening + 2 rebuttal + judge + carry-forward) to 3 (2 parallel attacker calls that each emit one strongest typed objection + 1 "reinforce-and-judge" call that produces both absorbed texts, flags no-ops, and renders the residual-attack verdict). The 2-exchange rebuttal round and the tie-break ladder are cut as selection theater that dilutes the objection harvest. (3) MAKE THE VALUE SELF-VALIDATING with a REINFORCEMENT YIELD metric reported as the headline output: yield = substantive (non-no-op) absorptions on the survivor's lineage / total debates. The Ledger lists every objection ever raised with provenance {axis-source, type, absorbed-edit or "no-op: already robust"}, and the final summary leads with "your idea absorbed K of N strongest attacks and shrugged off J as already-handled" — turning the open question "does the bracket beat generate-8-pick-1?" into a number the run prints about itself. Keep the 8 divergent axes (they are the objection-diversity engine, not contestants) and the final gauntlet (now framed as the last Ledger entries). Cut: the 5-score table, quoted top/bottom lines, tie-break rules, JSON sidecar, panel-of-3 option — everything whose job was crowning a winner rather than hardening the survivor.
rationale: The skill's only defensible reason to exist over a flat ensemble is reinforcement-under-selection, yet v1 buries it: carry-forward happens after selection, can be a no-op, and is outweighed by an elaborate 5-criterion crowning ritual. Dual-carry-forward-then-judge makes reinforcement happen on every variant every round and makes "under selection" literal (selection ranks reinforced ideas), while collapsing the judge to residual-attack-surface puts the thesis at the center of the one decision that matters. The yield metric converts the project's biggest unvalidated assumption into a self-reported number, and cutting selection theater both sharpens focus and halves cost.
selfWeakness: Replacing the five-criterion scored judge with a single "smaller residual attack surface" verdict removes the most legible audit trail and concentrates even more weight on one same-model judge call per pair — if that judge systematically conflates "hardest to attack" with "blandest/narrowest," the bracket will drift toward defensive, low-ambition survivors despite the typed-objection guardrail.

text:
Reframe idea-forge around three mechanism changes that make its output physically impossible to produce by flat-ensemble or by bake-to-completion, and that PROVE the difference rather than assert it.

(1) CROSSOVER carry-forward (genetic recombination, not patching). Replace "winner absorbs the loser's strongest objection" with: the winner must TRANSPLANT one concrete, named COMPONENT from the loser — a mechanism, feature, wedge, or framing the loser did better — AND neutralize the loser's strongest objection. The carry-forward agent returns a structured graft: {donor: #k, transplanted-component, before, after, why-it-survives}. The survivor therefore accumulates parts from every contender it beats. A "no-op" is forbidden at the component level: if nothing is worth transplanting, the judge must instead record an explicit "sterile round" flag (used later in the receipt). The final idea ships with a PROVENANCE LEDGER: each surviving component tagged with the contender # that authored it. Differentiation claim becomes observable: a flat ensemble returns exactly ONE unmodified variant; a valid forge output is a chimera citing >=3 distinct contributing contenders, or it self-reports failure.

(2) FALSIFIABLE BRACKET TEST (the anti-flat-ensemble guarantee, built in). Before the bracket, cheaply record the FLAT BASELINE = the judge's best-of-8 picked UNCHANGED (one extra judge call on the Round-0 set, no debates). After the final gauntlet, run exactly one blind head-to-head: forged-survivor vs flat-baseline, judged position-swapped and with provenance/edit-history stripped so the judge can't reward "the more-worked-on one." The skill only claims success and returns the forged idea if it wins this head-to-head by a stated margin; otherwise it returns the FLAT BASELINE and tells the user plainly "the bracket did not earn its cost on this idea — here is the cheaper answer, and here is why (sterile-round count, no-op rate)." This converts v1's unvalidated-bracket risk into a self-policing receipt: every run emits a measured forge-vs-flat delta. The bracket's reason to exist is tested on every invocation.

(3) DIVERGENCE GATE (anti-echo-chamber + sharpens the boundary vs bake). After Round 0, compute pairwise distinctness of the 8 variants (a quick judge pass scoring how structurally different each pair is, or self-reported axis-conflict). Any pair above a similarity threshold is collapsed and the slot re-spawned with an explicit "must contradict variant #x on its core mechanism" instruction. The bracket does not start until the field is provably divergent. This guarantees the adversarial debates are real, not echo.

BOUNDARY CONTRACT (added to SKILL as a decision table on OBSERVABLE outputs, not vibes): forge vs flat-ensemble — flat returns one untouched variant + a ranking; forge returns a recombined idea + provenance ledger + a measured forge-beats-flat receipt. forge vs bake-to-completion — bake is an OPEN-LOOP human interview that expands ONE idea conversationally with the user in the loop; forge is a CLOSED-LOOP, fully autonomous adversarial recombination of MANY rival versions with NO human turns after the single cost-confirm. Explicit handoff: bake emits a "forge-ready seed card" (idea + tagged load-bearing assumption + target user); forge consumes it and runs untouched to completion. If the user wants to be asked questions, that's bake, not forge. These three artifacts — provenance ledger, forge-vs-flat receipt, divergence-gate log — are the crisp, defensible line: none can be produced by either neighbor.
rationale: v1 only ASSERTS it differs from a flat ensemble ("carry-forward is the product"); a critic kills that because no-op edits collapse it back into selection. My changes make the difference measurable and self-falsifying: crossover produces a provably recombined output (provenance ledger citing >=3 contenders), and the built-in forge-vs-flat head-to-head emits a delta receipt every run, so the bracket must out-perform its own cheaper alternative or honestly return the flat answer. The divergence gate guarantees the debates are real rather than echo-chamber theater, and the boundary contract draws the line vs bake on observable output and human-in-loop, not branding.
selfWeakness: The forge-vs-flat head-to-head leans on the same single judge whose reliability is v1's weakest link — and that judge may exhibit edit/effort bias toward the more-elaborated forged text even after stripping provenance, so "forge beats flat" can be judge noise rather than real signal, partly undermining the very receipt I use to claim differentiation.

text: REPLACE THE BRACKET WITH A KING-OF-THE-HILL LADDER, AND COLLAPSE EACH "DEBATE" INTO A SINGLE STRUCTURED CALL. Concretely: (1) Generate contenders on cheaper models. Default N=5 most-orthogonal axes (assumption-attack, wedge, defensibility, radical-reframe, feasibility), lite N=3. Spawn them in parallel on Sonnet (optionally 1 Haiku) — NOT Opus. Same-model echo-chamber means independent Opus contenders never bought real diversity; a deliberate model-mix recovers more divergence for far less money. Each returns a strict fenced-JSON block (variant/rationale/self_weakness) so the orchestrator parses deterministically. (2) One Opus "seed-and-cull" call ranks all N on the rubric, names the top seed as CHAMPION, orders the rest as challengers in increasing-threat order, and drops any clearly dominated variant (cap the ladder at 4 challengers). This single call also serves as a cheap cross-model sanity check on the contenders. (3) Run a king-of-the-hill LADDER, not a bracket: the champion defends against each challenger in sequence. Each rung is ONE Opus call that does the whole debate-in-one: steelman BOTH sides (strongest case + strongest objection each way), score both on the 5-criterion rubric quoting the top/bottom line, pick the winner, name the loser's single strongest valid objection, AND emit the carry-forward edit (before/after/change or no-op flag) — all in one structured JSON return. The winner becomes the champion carried to the next rung. This makes reinforcement-carry-forward strictly LINEAR into one lineage, which is exactly the skill's stated core principle ("reinforcement under selection"); the bracket actually fought that principle by splitting reinforcement across two subtrees that only merge at the final. (4) Two hard cost-governors baked in: a BUDGET CAP (max Opus calls, surfaced in the cost gate as a single honest bounded number, e.g. "<=7 Opus + 5 Sonnet, hard-capped") and EARLY-STOP — if the carry-forward is a no-op for 2 consecutive rungs, terminate the ladder and report "idea is stable; tournament added little," which both saves calls and directly answers the unvalidated-bracket-value risk. (5) Spend the saved budget where it matters: the FINAL rung only (champion vs last surviving challenger) optionally upgrades to a true 2-independent-debater exchange, and the final gauntlet stays one Opus call. Net: full run drops from ~50 actual calls (v1's "24-30" undercounts — 7 debates x 6 calls + 8 contenders + gauntlet) to ~7 Opus + 5 cheap calls, sequential latency cut because each rung is 1 call not 6, with a fully deterministic, schema-validated, bounded execution path.
rationale: The dominant costs in v1 are the 6-calls-per-debate structure and using Opus for everything; collapsing each debate into one structured judge-call and demoting contenders to Sonnet attacks both without touching the actual product, which is carry-forward reinforcement. The ladder is not just cheaper than the bracket — it is more faithful to the skill's own stated core principle of linear reinforcement under selection, and it makes the cost number honest and hard-bounded. Early-stop on consecutive no-ops plus a budget cap convert the unvalidated-bracket-value risk into a self-limiting mechanism that spends only as much as the idea's instability warrants, and concentrating the one expensive true-debate at the final rung allocates rigor where the decision is highest-stakes.
selfWeakness: Collapsing the debate into a single judge-call sacrifices genuine independent adversarial exchange: the objections that feed carry-forward are now generated by the same agent that scores, risking weaker, self-serving critiques — and the king-of-the-hill ladder introduces champion/order bias that the bracket avoided.

text: **idea-forge v2 — Objection-Ledger + Bias-Audited Panel.** Stop treating the tournament as "judge crowns a winner, winner carries forward." Treat it as evidence-gathering whose payload is a deduplicated **objection ledger**, and make the judge mechanical and self-checking. Concrete changes: (1) **Consensus Auditor (echo-chamber fix).** After the 8 variants generate, one agent extracts the assumptions ALL variants silently share — the same-model fingerprint — and injects the top 1-2 as MANDATORY attack vectors every debate must address. Unanimous unexamined assumptions are exactly where correlated priors hide, so we attack agreement, not just differences. (2) **Decomposed, position-swapped, anchored scoring replaces the holistic judge.** Instead of one Opus call forming a global preference (halo/length/self-preference bias), spawn one narrow grader per criterion (Impact, Feasibility, Robustness, Defensibility, Clarity), each scoring A and B on ONLY its criterion against a pre-committed 1-5 rubric anchor and a MANDATORY quoted line. Run two passes with A/B order swapped. Winner = aggregate of swapped passes. **If the verdict flips under swap, or graders split, mark the debate LOW-CONFIDENCE:** order/bias decided it, so do not let it stand — both sides' objections go to the ledger and the bracket-advancer is chosen by Robustness-only, flagged in the transcript. This converts "is the judge reliable?" from a hope into a measured per-debate confidence signal. (3) **Objection Ledger replaces winner-only carry-forward (de-risks the path).** Every exchange yields candidate objections; a cheap single-purpose validity gate admits those that are grounded-in-the-text, unrebutted, and non-duplicate, keyed by the assumption they attack. Objections from *eliminated* variants are kept — a round-1 loser's killer point still lands. The bracket still advances a scaffold idea, but the FINAL idea is the surviving scaffold systematically amended to neutralize the top-K non-redundant ledger objections across the *whole* run, ranked by (graders-who-found-it-decisive × unrebutted). The survivor's quality no longer hinges on the judge having ranked correctly at each node. (4) **Built-in flat-vs-bracket A/B (attacks the unvalidated assumption directly).** Since 8 variants and panel scores already exist, also emit the free "generate-8-pick-1" flat answer (highest-aggregate variant, unamended) and report the explicit DELTA: which pivotal final changes came from ledger objections the flat pick would have missed. If delta ≈ 0, the run says so honestly — the skill now carries its own validation instead of asserting bracket superiority. (5) **Persona diversity on graders/auditor** (target-user, skeptic-investor, builder) so the panel isn't one prior; disagreement is surfaced as output, not averaged away. Lite mode: collapse to 2 graders (Impact+Robustness) but KEEP swap-validation and the ledger — those are the assumption-killers, not optional polish.
rationale: The two named assumptions are load-bearing only because v1 makes a single holistic same-model judge the arbiter and routes all value through the one surviving path. Swap-validation turns judge reliability into a detectable, flaggable per-debate signal instead of a silent dependency; decomposed anchored graders strip the global-preference biases that make same-model judging fragile. The objection ledger removes the bracket's path-dependence (good objections survive their author's elimination), and the built-in flat-vs-bracket delta converts the unvalidated "bracket beats ensemble" claim into a measured quantity the user sees every run. The Consensus Auditor attacks the echo chamber at its actual signature: unanimous unexamined assumptions.
selfWeakness: The final ledger-synthesis step (amend the scaffold to absorb top-K objections) is itself one unaudited holistic generation with the same single-model reliability concerns I claim to eliminate — I move the trust point rather than fully removing it — and the per-criterion × position-swap grading multiplies call count well past v1's already-high cost, weakening the "expensive, confirm first" promise.

text: Reforge idea-forge from a single-point hardener into a leverage-multiplying "decision frontier" engine, keeping carry-forward as the load-bearing mechanic but harvesting everything the bracket currently throws away. Five concrete mechanism changes: (1) DUAL-LEAGUE FRONTIER instead of 8->1 collapse. Split the 8 contenders into two basins by stance — a Convergent league (sharpen-value, feasibility, differentiation, defensibility) and a Divergent league (assumption-attack, reframe, expand-opportunity, wedge). Each runs its own 4->2->1 sub-bracket with full carry-forward (3 debates each = 6), then ONE cross-league championship debate whose output is NOT elimination but a CONDITIONAL VERDICT: both champions are kept and hardened, and the judge states the explicit assumptions/conditions under which the safe bet beats the bold bet. Total debates = 7, identical to v1 cost, but the run returns a decision-ready safe-bet + bold-bet pair instead of one point — and forcing two basins structurally fights the same-model echo-chamber collapse. (2) REINFORCEMENT LEDGER as a first-class reusable artifact. Every distinct valid objection + its repair edit across all rounds is accumulated into a structured Objection Ledger that ships as a standalone risk-register / kill-list the user reuses in pitches and plans — turning transcript exhaust into a deliverable. (3) OPPORTUNITY-EXPANSION ROUND (the axis applied to the skill itself): after champions are set, spawn 1-2 agents to produce an Expansion Map — the 3 highest-leverage adjacent extensions (new segment, new surface, new business-model) plus wedge-now->expand-later sequencing, each extension tagged to the specific Ledger assumption it depends on. One hardened idea becomes a hardened idea + a roadmap. (4) DOMAIN PACKS broaden WHERE it applies without diluting focus: the 5-criterion rubric is swappable — default Product pack, plus a Decision pack (swap Defensibility->Reversibility, add Option-value, for go/no-go, architecture, strategy) and a Research pack (swap Feasibility->Testability, Defensibility->Novelty, for hypotheses). Same machinery, much wider surface. (5) ADAPTIVE-DEPTH GATE + cross-run COMPOUNDING: a cheap divergence check after Round 0 measures how different the 8 variants actually are — near-duplicates auto-recommend collapsing to lite or stopping (right-sizing cost so the tool is safe to reach for on smaller ideas), high divergence justifies the full bracket; and the Ledger persists to ./idea-forge-runs/_ledger.md so later runs in the same project seed contenders and gauntlets with prior validated objections, making each successive run cheaper and sharper. Net cost ~v1 + ~4 calls; value per run multiplies (two hardened bets + reusable risk register + expansion roadmap + conditional decision guidance), and the addressable surface widens from 'one product idea post-bake' to any high-stakes proposition.
rationale: My axis is leverage-per-run and breadth-of-applicability, so I attacked the core waste: v1 spends 24-30 Opus calls to discard 7 variants and emit a single point, with all the harvested objections buried in a transcript. The dual-league frontier, Objection Ledger, and expansion round convert that discarded work into a decision-ready pair, a reusable risk register, and a roadmap at near-flat cost (7 debates, same as v1), while domain packs and the adaptive-depth gate widen where the tool is worth reaching for. Crucially I preserve focus by keeping carry-forward non-negotiable and additive rather than replacing the hardening core.
selfWeakness: Scope creep: a critic argues I diluted the crisp "carry-forward is the product" thesis into a do-everything suite, and that returning TWO hardened champions plus a roadmap risks giving the user indecision (two answers = no answer) instead of one sharpened commitment.

text: Rename to a single sharp job: "Sharpen-One" — cheaply, reliably harden ONE already-clear idea and leave a paper trail of what almost killed it. Ruthlessly delete the entire tournament: no 8 contenders, no bracket, no head-to-head debates, no apples-to-oranges 5-criterion judge. Keep ONLY the mechanism the skill itself names as "the product" — reinforcement carry-forward — and concentrate all spend there. New mechanism: a FIXED 4-rung adversary ladder applied to the seed in place, chosen as the four attacks that most often kill real ideas: (A) the load-bearing assumption, (B) status-quo inertia / "why would they switch", (C) first-version feasibility reality, (D) fast-follower durability. Each rung is one Opus red-teamer + one Opus amender, with NO scoring judge. Per rung: (1) red-teamer returns the single strongest, most specific objection plus a concrete failure scenario; (2) amender rewrites the idea to neutralize it without diluting the core, OR explicitly concedes and scopes the constraint out; (3) a KEEP/REVERT gate — the move that replaces the bracket's selection pressure in its smallest essence: a single cheap checker compares pre-amend vs post-amend (same-lineage, near-identical texts) on ONE binary question — "strictly stronger, or did the bolt-on patch weaken the core/clarity?" KEEP or REVERT-AND-RESCOPE. This is the key reliability win: the known weakest link — a judge comparing two DIFFERENT ideas — is eliminated, because the gate now only ever compares an idea to its own immediate predecessor, a far more reliable same-vs-same call. Rung A alone may propose a one-time structural reframe; the gate decides adoption. Cost collapses to ~8-9 Opus calls (down from 24-30) with no bracket and no 7 judges. Output: the hardened idea + an "objection ledger" (per rung: objection, failure scenario, amendment, KEPT or CONCEDED) + before/after. The ledger IS the transcript. Add a MICRO mode: just rung A (the assumption attack) = the 60-second, ~2-call forge; and demote the full tournament to an optional rarely-needed "pro mode" reachable only on explicit request. The default, and the thing the skill is now about, is the wedge.
rationale: v1's own design notes flag the bracket as unvalidated and the cross-idea judge as the weakest link, yet both consume the bulk of the 24-30 calls; cutting them keeps the one validated asset (carry-forward) and quarters the cost. Replacing the 5-criterion two-different-ideas judge with a binary same-lineage KEEP/REVERT gate directly fixes judge reliability because comparing an idea to its own predecessor is a far easier, less bias-prone call. A fixed 4-rung ladder of the attacks that actually kill ideas nails one high-value use case — fast, cheap, reliable hardening of a clear idea — completely, instead of doing eight things adequately.
selfWeakness: By deleting multi-variant generation, Sharpen-One can only sharpen the idea you brought — it can't discover you brought the wrong idea via a structural reframe (v1's axis-4/8 "navigation not summarization" leap), so the one-time rung-A reframe escape hatch may be too weak to surface genuinely different framings.

text: Redesign idea-forge as an AUDITED tournament whose robustness comes from decoupling, blinding, and mechanical gates rather than trust. Four concrete mechanism changes. (1) Forced divergence with a hard gate: each Round-0 contender gets its axis PLUS a randomly drawn hostile constraint from a fixed pool (e.g. "a funded incumbent clones you in 90 days," "your load-bearing assumption is false," "zero distribution budget," "regulated/adversarial users"). After spawning, run a cheap pairwise near-duplicate check; any variant that restates the seed, goes off-axis, or is a semantic twin of another is collapsed and ONE re-spawn is issued with a perturbed constraint. This converts the "all 8 look alike" failure from a vibe-warning into a blocking gate, partially breaking shared-prior convergence even on one model family. (2) The judge becomes a SCORER, not a decider. The judge sees only blinded variant texts — A/B labels randomized per debate, rationale and self-weakness stripped — and scores against fixed numeric anchors (an explicit description of what a 1 vs a 5 looks like per criterion). The HARNESS, not the judge, computes the winner from the score sums, removing the judge's freedom to pick a winner inconsistent with its own numbers. Every quoted "top/bottom line" must pass a literal substring check against the variant; a fabricated quote VOIDS the judgment and forces a re-judge. Each debate is judged twice with A/B position swapped; if the winner flips, escalate to a 3-judge median panel. (3) A reinforcement ledger replaces honor-system carry-forward. Each edit is classified substantive vs no-op by a measured diff, and the run emits a BRACKET-VALUE score = fraction of rounds where a substantive reinforcement actually altered the survivor. If that score is low, the output states plainly: "this run behaved like a flat generate-N-pick-1; your survivor is essentially the strongest Round-0 variant" — turning the unvalidated-bracket risk into a transparency metric the user can act on. A standing RED-TEAM agent (separate from contenders, purely destructive incentive, never competing to win) supplies one common kill-shot objection injected into every round, so reinforcement targets the idea's real throat, not just whatever the losing sibling happened to say. (4) A misuse-resistant operator contract: a preflight that hard-requires one seed, a logged verbatim user go-ahead with the stated call count, and a reproducibility header (model IDs, RNG seed for pairings/label assignment, prompt-version hash) before ANY spawn. Schema validation on every agent return with exactly one re-spawn on malformed output then graceful abort; explicit bye handling for collapsed brackets; and the Red-Flags table promoted from advice to enforced gates (skip carry-forward, skip the gauntlet, or judge-by-vibe are mechanically impossible, not merely discouraged).
rationale: v1's three named weak links — echo chamber, single unreliable judge, and unvalidated bracket value — are all trust-based, so the durable fix is to replace trust with mechanism: blind+anchor+swap+substring-check makes judging auditable and bias-detecting; the harness-computed tally and quote-void path remove the judge's two failure modes (incoherent picks, hallucinated evidence); the bracket-value score makes the "is this just a flat ensemble?" question self-reporting instead of hoping carry-forward mattered. The constraint pool plus duplicate-collapse gate and a non-competing red-team attack same-model correlation structurally rather than with a disclaimer, and the reproducibility header plus enforced gates make operator shortcuts impossible rather than merely warned against.
selfWeakness: Every robustness gate I add — duplicate-collapse re-spawns, position-swapped double-judging, panel escalation, the standing red-team — increases the already-high cost and latency v1 flagged, and the real echo-chamber cure (mixing model families) is unavailable, so blinding and constraints only dampen, not eliminate, shared priors; meanwhile the harness-computed tally is only as trustworthy as the judge's anchor calibration, which I assert but cannot guarantee.

text:
REFRAME: kill the bracket. Hardening an idea is not a sports tournament between rival paragraphs — it is driving ONE idea to an adversarial fixed point against an accumulating threat ledger. Replace single-elimination with a SIEGE LOOP whose central artifact is a persistent, deduplicated objection ledger that nothing ever falls out of.

Mechanism:
(0) Intake/cost gate sets a severity threshold (kill/wound/scratch; default repair >= wound) and a budget cap (default 3 waves).
(1) MOVE QUARRY — fan out the same ~8 axis agents, but they return discrete reusable MOVES (atomic design changes + the specific problem each solves), not whole rival ideas. These are ingredients, not competitors, so none are discarded.
(2) SYNTHESIS v0 — one builder assembles the strongest single champion by set-cover: pick a compatible subset of moves that maximizes value coverage, logging which moves it used/dropped and why. This replaces "pick the winner of 8."
(3) SIEGE WAVE (the engine) — spawn a red-team PANEL of diverse STAKEHOLDER PERSONAS in parallel (Skeptical Target User, Best-funded Competitor, Regulator/Compliance, CFO/unit-economics, Build Engineer, Future-at-100x, Disgruntled Power User). Personas, not abstract axes, force genuinely divergent objections from one model family — the direct fix for echo chamber. Each returns ranked objections tagged with severity and the assumption it exploits. Merge into the LEDGER: cluster near-duplicates, keep highest severity per cluster, status OPEN.
(4) TRIAGE + RESOLVE — a VERIFIER makes a BINARY judgment per objection (valid / out-of-scope), not a fragile 5-criteria comparison of two paragraphs. This is the key judge-reliability fix: verifying "is this objection real and is it resolved?" is far more auditable than "which idea is better." For each valid objection >= threshold, a repair agent amends the champion (pulling unused quarry moves where useful); status -> RESOLVED (with the fix delta), ACCEPTED-RISK (unfixable, logged honestly), or DEFERRED (below threshold).
(5) FIXED-POINT CHECK — re-siege the amended champion; objections append to the SAME ledger so nothing is lost across iterations. Stop when a full wave yields zero new valid >= threshold objections (hardened = fixed point reached), or cap hit, or new-objection count < k (diminishing returns). Cost is now ADAPTIVE: tight ideas converge in one wave and the tool says so; fragile ideas earn deeper sieges — you pay proportional to fragility.
(6) COHERENCE GUARD + COMPRESSION — every repair passes a "still one sharp, coherent idea" check, and a final compression pass forces the champion back to a tight paragraph, demoting low-value bolt-ons to ACCEPTED-RISK rather than features (anti-Frankenstein).
OUTPUT: hardened idea + the full LEDGER (every objection, source persona, severity, status, fix) + before/after + the residual ACCEPTED-RISK list = an explicit "what could still kill this." The ledger strictly dominates a bracket map. Lite mode: 4 quarry moves, 4 personas, 1 wave + 1 fixed-point check.
rationale: The bracket's three structural flaws — it discards 7/8 of generated material, its winner is chosen by a fragile paragraph-vs-paragraph comparison, and its depth is fixed regardless of how hard the idea already is — all dissolve under a ledger-driven siege. Accretion means every objection and every move persists and compounds instead of being eliminated; reframing the judge from comparison to binary verification makes the weakest link auditable per-objection; stakeholder personas manufacture real attack diversity from one model family; and the fixed-point termination makes cost proportional to fragility, which also self-validates the tool (a one-wave convergence honestly tells the user their idea was already hard).
selfWeakness: Set-cover synthesis plus per-objection repairs risks a bloated Frankenstein idea that addresses everything and commits to nothing, losing the sharp single vision the bracket's "one variant wins" naturally preserves — and repairs can spawn new objections, so the fixed point may oscillate and only halt via the budget cap rather than genuine convergence; the coherence guard and compression pass mitigate but do not fully guarantee this.
